update:
Brands data available in new countries 
 Brands data  is supported in the United States (US), Canada (CA), United Kingdom (GB), and Australia (AU).

New  brand_ids  filter for Places Count functions 
 Added a new  brand_ids  filter parameter to the  PLACES_COUNT_PER_H3 ,  PLACES_COUNT_V2 , and  PLACES_COUNT_PER_TYPE_V2  functions. You can use this parameter to filter counts by one or more specific brands.

Supported place types table available directly in BigQuery 
 The complete list of supported place types is available as a table directly in each Places Insights listing.
You can query this table directly to automate your workflows and instantly verify available types.

Announcing new Place Count functions 
 Announcing two new Place Count functions:  PLACES_COUNT_V2  and  PLACES_COUNT_PER_TYPE_V2 . These new functions offer significant performance improvements and support for batch processing of geographies.

New countries available 
 Places Insights data is now available for 36 new countries: Argentina, Austria, Bahrain, Belgium, Bulgaria, Chile, Colombia, Czechia, Denmark, Egypt, Finland, Greece, Hong Kong, Hungary, Ireland, Israel, Malaysia, Netherlands, New Zealand, Norway, Peru, Philippines, Poland, Portugal, Qatar, Romania, Saudi Arabia, Singapore, South Africa, South Korea, Sweden, Taiwan, Thailand, Turkey, United Arab Emirates, and Vietnam. 
 For a full list of supported countries and their schemas, see  Country-specific schema reference .

New business status 
 Added the  FUTURE_OPENING  business status for a business that is not yet operationalized, but is coming within the next 90 days.

New place types 
 Places Insights now supports 180 new types, which can be used to filter results. 
 For a complete list of supported types, see  Place Types (New) . Types added in this release are marked with an asterisk (*).
The  PLACES_COUNT_PER_H3  function now supports H3 resolutions up to 11 (previously 8).  See the documentation .

Places Count functions are now generally available.  Place count functions are predefined SQL queries that allow you to get and filter place counts per geography, type, and H3. You can also retrieve Place IDs to look up individual POI information.  See the documentation .

Announcing the Experimental release of  Historical data for Places Insights .
Historical data allows you to analyze how points of interest have changed
over time.

Full data listings 
are now available for all supported countries.

Brands data  is now available for
the entire United States.

Places Insights is now in General Availability . Use  this
form 
to sign up for Places Insights.

The Places Insights datasets have moved from data clean rooms to BigQuery data
exchange listings.  See the migration
guide .

Address fields in the dataset schema separate the  id  and  name  fields into
discrete string or string array fields.  See the migration
guide .

For Places Count functions, reduced the minimum search area from 500 meters by
500 meters (250,000 m 2 ) to 40.0 meters by 40.0 meters (1600 m 2 ).

The  PLACES_COUNT_PER_TYPE ,  PLACES_COUNT_PER_GEO , and  PLACES_COUNT_PER_H3 
functions now support the  sample_place_ids  column in the response. This
column, of type  ARRAY<STRING> , includes up to 250 place IDs for each element
in the response.

Announcing the  Experimental  release of  Place count functions  for Places Insights. Place count functions are predefined SQL queries that run in BigQuery and don't enforce a minimum count threshold. These functions are complementary to  places data queries  and the underlying data for both the dataset and the functions are the same.

Place names and granular address data such
as house number, road names, and zip codes are not available in the datasets.
Announcing the  Preview  release of Places Insights.
